name: Cuscusu Di Pistachi
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pan for steaming the couscous by mixing the flour and water together in a bowl and scraping onto a floured work surface. Knead briefly to form a rough, sticky dough, adding a tablespoon or two of flour if necessary.
2. Roll into a rope as long as the circumference of the bottom pan and apply the dough to the rim of the pan. Add water to the pan so that the water is below the bottom of the couscousiere or colander and press the couscousiere into place.
3. Bring the water to a boil on high heat until steam is escaping freely through the perforations, then lower to a simmer so that the steam escapes very gently.
4. While the water is coming to a boil, prepare the couscous by placing it in a bowl and adding water. Swish the water through the grain with the fingers of one hand splayed apart, raking through it. Tilt the bowl and drain away excess water. Set aside for 5 minutes.
5. Line the couscousiere with a dampened napkin or cheesecloth and add the grain. Cover and steam for 15 minutes.
6. While the grain is cooking, prepare the flavoring by half filling a saucepan with water and adding pistachios. Bring to a boil; drain in a strainer. Pour the pistachios onto a towel, fold the towel over them, and rub to loosen skins. Separate pistachios from skins carefully.
7. Combine the freshly blanched pistachios, almond extract, and cinnamon in a food processor bowl and process for 2-3 minutes until very finely ground and starting to become pasty. Add almond oil (or mild vegetable oil) one tablespoon at a time and continue processing mixture to a smooth paste, stopping the machine and scraping the inside of the bowl three or four times.
8. After the couscous has steamed for 15 minutes, remove it from the couscousiere in the cheesecloth and put it in a large, non-reactive roasting pan. Spread it out with a fork and let cool.
9. Combine salt and water and work it into the cooled couscous, raking through with one hand. Add the pistachio paste in three or four additions, working it in by rubbing it and the couscous together with fingertips.
10. Return the water in the bottom pan to a simmer and line the couscousiere again with the dampened cheesecloth. Add the seasoned couscous to the couscousiere, cover, and steam for 15 minutes.
11. Remove the couscous to the roasting pan, spread out with a fork without compressing the mixture, and leave uncovered until cool.
12. To finish the couscous, bring sugar and water to a boil, stirring to dissolve all sugar granules, then cool the syrup. Work the cooled couscous between palms of hands again to separate grains and toss in five or six additions with the syrup, fluffing the couscous with a fork.
13. Allow the couscous to dry uncovered several hours in a cool place, fluffing it occasionally until all the grains are separate. Cover tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate up to several days.
14. To serve, fluff up with a fork so the grains separate well and mound the couscous on a platter. Sprinkle evenly with grated chocolate, then confectioners' sugar. Decorate with no more than five or six pieces of candied fruit.
